title: Onagra hookeri
taxonomy: Onagra hookeri, also known as Hooker's evening primrose, belongs to the family Onagraceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as willow-herbs. Onagraceae is further divided into 22 genera, with Onagra being one of them. The genus Onagra is a group of annual or biennial plants native to North America. Within the genus Onagra, Hooker's evening primrose is one of the species native to western North America.
uses: Onagra hookeri, also known as Hooker's evening primrose, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, Hooker's evening primrose is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for relieving joint pain and swelling. The plant's roots are also used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Hooker's evening primrose for medicinal purposes.
